codeReview.summary: The feature implements most required SREEnvironment lifecycle behaviors, but there is a blocking correctness issue in incident tracking after remediation.

issues.description: state.active_incidents always includes entries from self._chaos.get_active_faults(), but bad_config faults remain in the chaos engine after rollback remediation. Repro: inject bad_config via env._chaos._inject_specific_fault(...), run Rollback on the same service, then env.state.active_incidents still contains 'bad_config on <service>' even though health has recovered. This violates expected behavior that active_incidents reflect current faults and that correct remediation resolves faults.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The env-worker skill procedure is too generic about expected-behavior verification and did not ensure explicit validation of incident lifecycle after remediation (fault appears, then disappears).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/env-worker/SKILL.md step 2 says to cover expectedBehavior, but tests/test_environment.py:483-491 (rollback case) only asserts recovered health_status, not that env.state.active_incidents clears the incident.

summary: Reviewed feature sre-environment (commit 1721e31) and found a blocking issue: remediated bad_config faults can remain reported as active incidents due to stale chaos fault tracking. Report marked fail.
